summary: Reporter says a Blazor WASM app started throwing System.DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp after moving from .NET 9 to .NET 10. They tested SkiaSharp 3.116.1 and 3.119.2-preview1 and linked a repro repository. A follow-up comment marks this report as a duplicate of #3422.

bugSignals.stackTrace: Not provided in #3435. Related issue #3422 includes: System.DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp at SkiaSharp.SKPath..ctor.

stepsToReproduce: Upgrade an existing Blazor WebAssembly app from .NET 9 to .NET 10.
Run with SkiaSharp packages (reporter tried 3.116.1 and 3.119.2-preview1).
Navigate to usage path that initializes SkiaSharp and observe System.DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp.
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Blazor WebAssembly on .NET 10; no OS version details supplied.

versionAnalysis.brokeIn: Same package set after moving to .NET 10
versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: ai-repro/3422.json reports not-reproduced on .NET SDK 10.0.102 with wasm-tools installed, while issue comments still show user reports of failures in other environments.

regression.reason: The reporter observed a break after a .NET SDK/platform upgrade rather than after a SkiaSharp package update, and #3422 reproduction could not reproduce on newer SDK/tooling.

fixStatus.reason: No merged SkiaSharp fix is linked for #3435; related repro for #3422 is not-reproduced on newer SDKs, but ongoing reports indicate unresolved environment-specific failures.

analysis.summary: This report matches the same symptom cluster tracked in #3422: Blazor WASM + .NET 10 can surface libSkiaSharp load failures in some setups. Code inspection shows Blazor interop still P/Invokes libSkiaSharp and WebAssembly native linking is handled through NativeFileReference selection for net9.0+ static libraries, so failures are likely toolchain/environment-dependent instead of a unique code-path bug in #3435. Because #3422 already contains broader cross-platform evidence and a dedicated repro artifact, this issue should be consolidated as a duplicate.
analysis.rationale: Type is bug because the reporter sees a runtime exception that blocks rendering. Area is SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor because the path is specific to Blazor WebAssembly interop and WASM native linkage, not desktop native asset loading. Platform is WASM (not Windows-specific), and compatibility is the main tenet because the trigger is the .NET 9→10 transition. Duplicate handling is appropriate since #3435 has the same signature and was explicitly linked to #3422 in-thread.

keySignals.text: Started getting this runtime error after upgrading Blazor app from .Net 9 to .Net 10.
Not working, runtime error System.DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp
keySignals.interpretation: Primary failure signature is native library resolution/linking.
Reporter/community already linked this to an existing tracking issue.

keySignals.text: Could not reproduce on macOS arm64 with .NET SDK 10.0.102 and wasm-tools workload installed
keySignals.interpretation: Suggests environment/toolchain variability and supports consolidation rather than fragmenting reports.

codeInvestigation.finding: Blazor interop declares [DllImport("libSkiaSharp")] for InterceptBrowserObjects(), so missing/failed native linkage surfaces as libSkiaSharp load exceptions in this path.
WASM projects set WasmBuildNative=true and select static .a files via NativeFileReference, using emscripten 3.1.56 binaries for TargetFrameworkVersion >= 9.0.
SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor includes a project reference to SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly outside VS builds, confirming native assets are wired through the Blazor package path.
Docs specify SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor auto-includes WebAssembly native assets and WASM uses static linking (not dynamic runtime deployment), matching the observed error class.

workarounds: Ensure WebAssembly build tools are installed and restored (dotnet workload restore / dotnet workload install wasm-tools).
Use a current stable .NET 10 SDK and retest; related repro for #3422 succeeded on 10.0.102.
Track ongoing updates in #3422 instead of splitting diagnostics across duplicate threads.
nextQuestions: For still-affected environments, what does dotnet --info show outside repos with global.json?
Does dotnet workload list show wasm-tools installed for the active SDK feature band?

actionability.reason: #3435 matches #3422 symptom and context, and #3422 already has broader investigation plus a dedicated reproduction artifact.

actions.description: Align labels with WASM Blazor compatibility tracking
Link this issue as duplicate of #3422
Post duplicate-routing note with immediate workaround context
actions.comment:
Thanks for the report. This appears to be the same issue tracked in #3422 (Blazor WASM on .NET 10 with intermittent libSkiaSharp load failures), so consolidating there will keep investigation in one place.

Immediate workaround that helps in several reports: ensure WASM tools are installed/restored for the active SDK (dotnet workload restore or dotnet workload install wasm-tools), then retest on the latest .NET 10 SDK.

Closing this as a duplicate of #3422 so updates stay centralized.
